query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "opencomputer: verifiable software worlds for computer-use agents" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it provides empirical evidence on the same mechanism (computer-use agents performing verifiable software tasks) and measures the dependent variable (agent success rates on GUI-based workflows) within the specific domain of software automation, serving as a canonical benchmark study for the "OpenComputer" framework's application.
